title: What Makes a Voice Recognizable

analysis.object: T643: What Makes a Voice Recognizable depth analysis

fundamental_eml3.description: Fundamental frequency: EML-3 oscillatory (pitch = periodic vibration)
fundamental_eml3.reason: Voice pitch is EML-3: fundamental frequency is EML-3 oscillatory; measured in Hz

formants_eml2.description: Formant ratios: EML-2 measurement of resonance frequencies
formants_eml2.reason: Formants are EML-2: discrete resonance peaks measured in spectrogram

timbre_emlinf.description: Vocal timbre: EML-inf; no finite set of measurements fully captures unique voice
timbre_emlinf.reason: Voice recognition is EML-inf: individual vocal identity is beyond finite spectral description

synthetic_wrong.description: Synthetic voices sound wrong: reproduce EML-3 oscillation without EML-inf individuality
synthetic_wrong.reason: TTS fails EML-inf: EML-3 oscillation correct but EML-inf timbre absent; voice feels hollow

analysis.theorem: T643: What Makes a Voice Recognizable (S922).

key_theorem: T643: What Makes a Voice Recognizable (S922).
